parameters: Demon:
Looks like this shelf's full of books on tactics. I bet there are
lots of things you don't understand about battles, right?
Demon:
It might be a good idea to do some research on how humans fight.
Demon:
I'm in charge of looking after this library, but now that they've
decided to destroy it, I guess my life's about to get a lot easier.
Demon:
I actually liked being able to read all those books the humans wrote.
It's a shame.
[Do-or-Die]
The strongest enemies can go into a Do-or-Die state when their HP
drops low enough. You can't be too careful when this happens.
Do-or-Die gives the enemy an increased number of attacks per turn.
If they use too many strong attacks at once, you could end up being
the one in trouble.
They'll also become immune to status effects like Blind, Silence,
Confusion, Paralysis, and Sleep. You'll need to use your wits to
defeat these enemies.
A
This shelf contains children's books. It looks like you can read
through the tutorial again.
Would you like to go through the introductory tutorial again?
Yes

Demon:
Looks like this shelf's full of manuals. I bet you've come across
items you don't understand, right?
Demon:
The items humans make are pretty complicated. It's a pain,
but I guess I can prepare something to help you.
[Books]
Records describing the experiences of our predecessors are called
books. Reading one gives you a sizable chunk of XP.
This can help low-level characters catch up to the party or give
high-level characters a greater boost.
Stronger enemies don't give XP but drop books instead. Only common
enemies give XP when you defeat them. Know what that means?
If you skip fighting weak enemies and only go for the strong ones,
you're in for a world of pain. That'll probably appeal to those who
like a challenge, though.
パーシアス
[Holy Land Pacius]
Pacius was created at the beginning of the world. God created three
humans and left them there.
The three were called the Sages and shared their insight with the
ever-growing human population. They taught mankind how to
use words, how to hunt, and how to build their own homes.
The Sages are undying, constantly being reborn into the world.
Those who've lost their way go to Pacius to be guided by the
Sages to a happier life.
<AreaEvent:3x1>
クミン
[Neutral Republic of Kumin]
A country of absolute neutrality. Kumin has immense economic
power due to its advanced mithril-processing technology.
Being situated next to the Empire, Kumin has faced a great many
difficulties. At one point, it even came close to being wiped out.
Kumin's Radiant Tower is a popular tourist spot. It has
furniture on display that's over one thousand years old
and attracts over two million visitors per year.
[Adult Goods]
Are you all familiar with DoReMi Fuckingdom's unique equipment?
They looks like they're only good for indecent purposes,
but their goods are surprisingly useful.
It's very difficult to earn their special currency, but we recommend
your first purchases be whips and underwear.
Demon:
Looks like this shelf's full of history books. The world sure can
change a lot in a thousand years.
Demon:
It might be good to learn about the countries you're going to
conquer or have already conquered.
[Kingdom of Bohelos]
Saint Bohelos descended upon this unclean world and founded a
country to free the slaves.
With the support of many other countries, Bohelos managed to
reject the Empire's policy of slavery. Lace was later founded
and based its ideals upon those of Bohelos.
These peaceful ideals have survived to present day. The many
incredible citizens have maintained them through a multitude
of wars and natural disasters.
Once the country found peace, Saint Bohelos returned to the
Land of God.
[The Empire]
With a thousand years of history, the Empire is the largest country
on the continent. It presides over the laws of the other countries.
The Empire inspects the distribution of all goods, maintains records
of each person born, and controls everyone who becomes a slave.
Since the implementation of the Empire's oversight, the number of
wars has greatly diminished and the economy has grown stronger.
Mankind enjoys a peaceful world under the Empire's patronage.
Demon:
Read book. Romance. Human. And. Monster.
Demon:
Read book.
Demon:
Good part. Not interrupt.
Demon:
Read book. Soon cannot read. Read now.
[R-18 Skip Mode]
Turn R-18 Skip Mode ON from the Options menu to hide all of the
sex scenes in the game.
Even with R-18 Skip Mode ON, unlocked scenes will still be available
in the recollection area. You can view them there.
It's designed for people who prefer to focus on the story or don't
want to worry about friends and family looking over their shoulder.
[Achievements]
When you accomplish something big, you get an achievement.
They're medals for those who've overcome challenges.
Still, they're purely decorative. They don't provide any bonuses.
The joy of possessing them is all you get.
[Attributes - Volume 2]
To make an attack with an attribute, you need to use magic with
that attribute to attack... Or an attributed weapon.
There's a low chance of getting an attributed weapon as a drop from
special monsters. Try farming the same monsters over and over to
make things easier down the road.
[Attributes - Volume 1]
There are many types of attributes. Fire, water, lightning... Use them
to capitalize on an enemy's weakness for an easier fight.
Enemies from the same area often share the same weaknesses.
For example, enemies from Tortas tend to be weak to water.
You can find out what the local enemies are weak to by asking
around. Intelligence-gathering is an essential part of warfare.
[Attacks and Skills]
Skills are very useful in battle, but sometimes normal attacks are
the way to go.
If your skills don't seem to be doing much damage to the enemy,
try checking your equipment or using normal attacks instead.
[Guard]
This skill makes one character protect the others. It's best used by
someone with high defense.
It doesn't always have to be the hardiest character who uses it,
though. Don't be reluctant to try new things. It's always worth
testing a different approach.
[Back Attack]
If you touch the back of an enemy that hasn't spotted you,
you'll initiate a Back Attack.
Back Attacks give you an advantage by forcing the enemy to fight
while unprepared and confused.
You also get better drop rates from battles that start with a Back
Attack. Be sure to use it when you're aiming for rare items that
have lower drop rates.
[Attributes - Volume 3]
If you think of human as being an attribute, does that mean there
are attacks that works against all humans? The answer is yes.
There are certain weapons and skills that are extra effective against
humans. There may even be an attribute all humans are weak to.
[Buffs and Debuffs]
Buffs are status effects that strengthen your party members.
Debuffs are status effects that weaken your enemies.
Buffs can increase your party's Attack and Defense. Both skills and
magic can be used to buff the party. It's simple, but you'll see the
value in it if a battle drags on.
Debuffs can lower your enemy's Attack and Defense. Both skills
and magic can be used to debuff enemies. The stronger the foe,
the more value you'll get out of them.
[Maximum Number of Items]
You can carry up to 99 of any human-made item, Healing Water,
and Reviver.
For natural items like Sakura Leaves and Spirit Stones,
you can carry up to 9,999 of each.
Items may have the same effect, but the number that can be carried
speaks to their utility. Ones that are plentiful should be used on the
map, while those that are few in number are best saved for battle.
[Kingdom of Elegance, Lace]
The Silver Knight, Lace, wanted to found a country of peace in
accordance with Saint Bohelos's teachings.
Using their special, non-lethal spearmanship and diplomacy, Lace
managed to triumph over their foes. Their style of battle became
a hot topic across the continent.
In recent years, due to the large number of battles it's faced and
strong domestic demand, Lace has formed an order of knights.
There's a note at the end of the book:
Lace has lost its territory and is no longer a country.
猿の王国の前身
[Kingdom of Blades, Yaldapa]
A country with a long history, famous for its characteristic swords.
Known for both its famous swords and strong swordsmanship.
Many of Yaldapa's queens have been master swordswomen,
revered by their people. The sword dance they perform at
the nation's festivals is admired throughout the continent.
Cooking knives made with Yaldapa's smithing techniques are in high
demand all over the continent. Every household has a Yaldapa knife.
The weight of the blade alone can cut tomatoes into thin slices.
There's a note at the back of the book:
Yaldapa has lost its territory and is no longer a country.
マーク６の前身
[Kingdom of Lohba]
A verdant nation situated next to the Ancient Lake. Perhaps due to
the majesty of the lake, the people have very calm temperaments.
Sandwiched between Rooklook's walls and the Ancient Lake,
they're very tenacious in battle. Lohba has a reputation as
a small but impregnable country.
A note at the end of the book says:
Lohba has lost its territory and is no longer a country.
A
None of these books are of any use.
[Ultimate Skill Cut-In Skip]
When you spend all your time battling, efficiency becomes key.
Having to sit through too many animations is a real drag.
So skip them! Use as many Ultimate Skills as you like without
having to sit through the animation every time!
You can't read these titles yet.
[Bad End]
When you fall in battle, you can select the Bad End option.
This will let you see the horrible end the characters face.
If you're brave enough, you might even choose to see the tragic
truth of this world.
Sarasa:
Freedom beyond the wall... I feel more anxious every day, but...
Sarasa:
That anxiety just makes me want to become stronger!
I'll become strong, just like Polca!
Micchan:
We studied so hard and for so long... Does that not mean anything
out here in the wider world?
Student:
I trusted that everything I learned in class was the truth.
Now I know it was whatever suited the adults the most.
Scenario 03bohe_main07
A
[Luck]
Are you looking down on items and equipment that increase your
luck? Don't you think it's important to have good luck?
Luck is strongly connected to status effects. Higher luck means a
higher chance of inflicting Faint or of your party members being
able to resist Paralysis in battle.
You lose nothing by increasing your luck, so get out there and find
yourself more of it!
Oh? There's something at the back of the shelf.
A
Lucky!
[Luck]
Are you looking down on items and equipment that increase your
luck? Don't you think it's important to have good luck?
Luck is strongly connected to status effects. Higher luck means a
higher chance of inflicting Faint or of your party members being
able to resist Paralysis in battle.
You lose nothing by increasing your luck, so get out there and find
yourself more of it!
You can't read these titles yet.
[Bad End - Volume 2]
Somewhere in the Slave Internment Camp is the Execution Dungeon.
The grisly images it conjures serve as a chain that binds the hearts
of slaves across the land.
On the off chance you end up there, investigate the slaves who
have fallen into despair. An unrealized tenacity sleeps there.
That tenacity may lead to something. For example, a mysterious doll
who's been entrusted with a mission.
[Return]
When you want to return quickly from a dungeon, you may find
Return Scrolls useful.
They're especially useful when dealing with deep dungeons or
dungeons that contain lots of powerful enemies. You'll always
want to carry at least one.
An even more useful item is the Hometown Ocarina. This item is
reusable, so if you find it, don't be afraid to use it.
A
The appendix contains a Return Scroll!
[Return]
When you want to return quickly from a dungeon, you may find
Return Scrolls useful.
They're especially useful when dealing with deep dungeons or
dungeons that contain lots of powerful enemies. You'll always
want to carry at least one.
An even more useful item is the Hometown Ocarina. This item is
reusable, so if you find it, don't be afraid to use it.
[Hidden Treasure]
There are hidden treasures in this world that have yet to be discovered.
If you manage to get your hands on them, they'll probably provide
incredible powers... if they truly exist.
[Healing Mist]
Back before there was magic, people thought they could heal their
injuries with herbs and inner spirit.
The strangest idea of all was that scents could heal. That by sniffing
certain herbs or musks, a person could heal their body.
The method of making it has been lost, but of all these treatments,
Healing Mist is by far the most effective. It has outstanding healing
properties, so be bold yet prudent when you have some.
[Battle Ocarina]
When you need to fight enemies over and over, this is where the
Battle Ocarina comes into play.
Play the Battle Ocarina and, even if you've cleared the current area
of enemies, a battle will start.
Use it to farm XP, drops, or anything else you need.
